site.title: Radical Responsibility
site.author: Lee Hahn
site.description: The Math of Relationships, Emotions, and Change. A new book by Lee Hahn on noticing, owning, and changing your patterns so your life actually shifts.

cta._comment: When the book launches, change buyLabel from 'Preorder the Book' to 'Buy the Book'. This updates the nav, hero, book section, and final CTA all at once.

hero.title: Radical
hero.subtitle: Will Change How You See Everything
hero.body: This isn't about fixing other people. It's about noticing, owning, and changing your patterns so your life actually shifts.

image.alt: Lee Hahn — Author of Radical Responsibility

framework.label: The Framework
framework.title: The math of change
framework.body: Behavior is both science and math. Once you see the equation, you can't unsee it — and you can finally start changing what hasn't worked. Radical Responsibility gives you the framework to stop reacting and start choosing.

pillars.title: Awareness
pillars.body: Most people are operating on autopilot. The first step is noticing your patterns — without judgment, without excuses.

pillars.title: Ownership
pillars.body: Ownership isn't blame. It's power. When you own your role in a situation, you become the one who can change it.

pillars.title: Action
pillars.body: Insight without action is just awareness. This is where the work becomes real — small, deliberate shifts that compound into lasting change.

whoFor.label: Who This Is For
whoFor.title: If you keep ending up
whoFor.titleItalic: in the same place, this is for you.

cards.title: People Pleasers
cards.body: You've spent years managing everyone else's comfort while quietly resenting it. There's another way to show up in relationships.

cards.title: The Emotionally Depleted
cards.body: You give everything to everyone and wonder why you feel empty. Understanding the math of your emotions changes everything.

cards.title: Pattern Repeaters
cards.body: Same relationship, different person. Same job, different company. Until you see the pattern, you can't break it.

cards.title: Self-Trust Seekers
cards.body: You second-guess every decision and look to others to tell you what's right. Radical Responsibility returns you to yourself.

cards.title: Growth-Oriented Minds
cards.body: You've read the books, done the work — but something still feels stuck. This framework gives that effort somewhere to land.

cards.title: Educators & Leaders
cards.body: You shape how others think, behave, and grow. Radical Responsibility gives you a framework that actually transfers to others.

items.title: Notice
items.body: Before you can change anything, you have to see it clearly. Notice teaches you to observe your patterns, reactions, and emotional triggers without the distortion of shame or defense.

items.title: Own
items.body: Ownership is the move that most people avoid — and the one that changes everything. Own your role in what's not working, and you take back the power to fix it.

items.title: Show Up
items.body: The final move is the most visible one. Show Up means acting in alignment with who you want to be — not who fear, habit, or expectation has shaped you into.

author.label: About Lee
author.name: Lee Hahn
paragraphs: Lee Hahn didn't arrive at Radical Responsibility through theory. She arrived through a moment that stopped her cold — a student in crisis, a response she didn't expect from herself, and a question she couldn't let go of: What is my role in this?
That question became a framework. That framework became a practice. And that practice — applied across years of work in education, relationships, and leadership — became this book.

Lee's work sits at the intersection of behavior science and emotional intelligence. She believes that responsibility isn't a burden — it's a superpower. And that the people willing to do the hard work of owning their patterns are the ones who actually change.
She writes for people who are tired of almost-there. Who want something that actually works.

book.label: The Book
book.title: Radical Responsibility
book.subtitle: The Math of Relationships, Emotions, and Change
paragraphs: Part framework, part field guide — Radical Responsibility gives you the tools to stop reacting to your life and start designing it. Through the lens of Empathy + Accountability = Responsibility, Lee Hahn makes the invisible visible: why you keep ending up where you don't want to be, and exactly what to do differently.
This isn't soft. It isn't a 30-day challenge. It's a shift in how you see yourself — and everyone else.

image.alt: Radical Responsibility by Lee Hahn — Book Cover

finalCta.title: Ready to see what you've been
finalCta.subtitle: The framework is simple. The shift is real.
